1.q: promoted BR synthesis through the RtNAC081–RtBZR1 axis, and also activated BR signaling

2.q: promoted BR synthesis through the RtNAC081–RtBZR1 axis... improved salt tolerance through physical interaction with RtNAC081

4.q: the BR–BZR1 signaling pathway plays a pivotal role in the regulation of plant growth and developmental processes

6.q: RtNAC081 activated RtAPX5 to modulate reactive oxygen species balance and photosynthetic efficiency, which ultimately improved salt tolerance.

8.q: RtNAC081 activated RtAPX5 to modulate reactive oxygen species balance and photosynthetic efficiency, which ultimately improved salt tolerance.

9.q: the promoter region of RtNCED1.1 contains a NAC binding site, suggesting that RtNCED1.1 is a target gene of RtNAC081... RtNAC081 activated RtNCED1.1 expression

10.q: RtVP1 confers salt tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis by maintaining Na+/K+ homeostasis and enhancing the antioxidant and osmotic regulatory capacity.

11.q: Transgenic plants overexpressing RtLDOX2 accumulated more anthocyanin and flavonols under abiotic stresses.

12.q: Two LDOX genes (RtLDOX and RtLDOX2) were significantly differentially expressed before and after salt stress, which showed very low homology and were on different branches of the phylogenetic tree.

13.q: An H+-pyrophosphatase gene (RtVP1) was isolated from R. trigyna which encoded a plasma membrane and tonoplast-localized protein.

14.q: R. trigyna RtVP1 ... confers salt tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis by maintaining Na+/K+ homeostasis and enhancing the antioxidant and osmotic regulatory capacity.

15.q: RtNAC081 alleviates salt stress in an RtNCED1.1-dependent manner; RtNAC081 activated RtNCED1.1 expression
